architecture_decisions.decision: Build Windows Agent as Go service using golang.org/x/sys/windows for Win32 API integration
architecture_decisions.rationale: Maintains language consistency with backend microservices, leverages existing internal/shared packages for auth/telemetry, Go provides excellent cross-compilation for Windows targets

architecture_decisions.decision: Use Windows Print Spooler API via WMI (Win32_Printer) for printer discovery and job submission
architecture_decisions.rationale: Native Windows API provides comprehensive printer access, supports 99%+ printer compatibility as identified in market analysis of PaperCut MF

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement bidirectional WebSocket communication via notification-service for real-time job updates
architecture_decisions.rationale: Eliminates polling latency, matches competitor (Printix) real-time capabilities, reduces bandwidth compared to continuous polling

architecture_decisions.decision: Use Wails (Go + WebView) for system tray UI instead of native Win32
architecture_decisions.rationale: Faster development cycle, easier to maintain, allows React component reuse from dashboard, modern UI appearance

architecture_decisions.decision: Store agent configuration in Windows Registry under HKLM\Software\OpenPrint with JSON fallback
architecture_decisions.rationale: Standard Windows practice for services, supports group policy deployment, enables silent installation pre-seeding

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement offline job queue with retry logic and exponential backoff
architecture_decisions.rationale: Addresses Printix weakness of limited offline capabilities, ensures print jobs complete when connectivity restored

backend_tasks.purpose: Define shared types for agent communication, printer discovery, and job status
Handle printer discovery registration from agents
Efficient job polling endpoint for agents
Enhanced heartbeat with session state, printer count, job queue depth
Agent-specific JWT authentication using machine certificates
Optimized document download for agents with resume support

frontend_tasks.purpose: Display all registered agents with status, last heartbeat, associated user
Detailed agent view with discovered printers, job history, health metrics
Show printers discovered by each agent with capabilities and status
Visual representation of agent uptime, job success rate, response time
Admin interface to assign print jobs to specific users/agents
View all agent-discovered printers across the organization
API client methods for agent-related operations
Reusable badge showing online/offline/errored agent status

database_migrations.sql:
CREATE TABLE agents (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    machine_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    hostname VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    agent_version VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    os_version VARCHAR(100),
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'online',
    last_heartbeat TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    session_state VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
    certificate_thumbprint VARCHAR(64),
    capabilities JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(hostname, user_id)
); CREATE INDEX idx_agents_user_id ON agents(user_id); CREATE INDEX idx_agents_status ON agents(status);

CREATE TABLE discovered_printers (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    share_name VARCHAR(255),
    port_name VARCHAR(100),
    driver_name VARCHAR(255),
    device_id VARCHAR(100),
    printer_type VARCHAR(20),
    connection_type VARCHAR(20),
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'unknown',
    capabilities JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    ip_address INET,
    last_seen TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(agent_id, device_id)
); CREATE INDEX idx_discovered_printers_agent_id ON discovered_printers(agent_id); CREATE INDEX idx_discovered_printers_status ON discovered_printers(status);

ALTER TABLE print_jobs ADD COLUMN assigned_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; ALTER TABLE print_jobs ADD COLUMN assigned_agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; ALTER TABLE print_jobs ADD COLUMN claimed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE; ALTER TABLE print_jobs ADD COLUMN agent_started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE; ALTER TABLE print_jobs ADD COLUMN agent_completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE; CREATE INDEX idx_print_jobs_assigned_user ON print_jobs(assigned_user_id, status); CREATE INDEX idx_print_jobs_assigned_agent ON print_jobs(assigned_agent_id, status);

CREATE TABLE agent_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    event_data JSONB,
    severity VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'info',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
); CREATE INDEX idx_agent_events_agent_id ON agent_events(agent_id, created_at DESC); CREATE INDEX idx_agent_events_type ON agent_events(event_type, created_at DESC);

CREATE TABLE agent_certificates (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    certificate PEM NOT NULL,
    thumbprint VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    issued_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    revoked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    revoked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
); CREATE INDEX idx_agent_certificates_thumbprint ON agent_certificates(thumbprint); CREATE INDEX idx_agent_certificates_agent_id ON agent_certificates(agent_id);

cases: GetPrinterCapabilities extracts duplex and color support
Failed download updates job status to failed
Printer cancellation stops job and updates status
Concurrent job processing respects queue order
Stale printer cleanup removes old entries
PollJobs returns only unclaimed jobs for user
ClaimJob marks job as claimed and prevents double claim
CompleteJob sets final status and timestamps
Session lock detection pauses job processing

steps: Start agent with test printers installed

steps: Create print job assigned to test user

e2e_tests.name: Agent Offline Queue
steps: Verify all jobs print in order

security_notes: Agent certificates must use X.509 with RSA 2048 or ECDSA P-256
All agent-to-service communication requires mutual TLS or signed JWT
Print documents must be encrypted in transit (HTTPS) and at rest on agent temp directory
Agent configuration in registry should be ACL-restricted to SYSTEM and Administrators
Windows service should run as NETWORK SERVICE or dedicated low-privilege account
Document download tokens must be short-lived (<5 minutes) and single-use
Agent must validate printer driver signatures before submitting jobs
Sensitive data (credentials, certificates) stored in Windows Credential Manager
Implement code signing for all agent executables and installers
Agent telemetry must not capture document contents or user data
docker_changes: Add agent compilation stage to Dockerfile.build
Add Windows-specific build environment with MinGW or cross-compiler
market_driven_features: Direct IP printing support to match Printix serverless architecture
Offline queue with retry to address Printix connectivity dependency weakness
Real-time WebSocket job delivery for instant printing
Comprehensive printer capability detection (color, duplex, paper sizes) matching PaperCut MF
Agent health dashboard with visual analytics matching YSoft SafeQ reporting
Silent installation with Group Policy support for enterprise deployment
Session-aware printing for security (matches secure print release patterns)
